summary: The conjecture that every graph $G$ with $\chi(G) \ge t$ immerses $K_t$ remains open in full generality. Post-posting, DeVos–Kawarabayashi–Mohar–Okamura (2010) confirmed it for $t \le 7$ via the stronger fact that minimum degree $t-1$ suffices; Gauthier–Le–Wollan (2017) showed chromatic number at least $3.54t+4$ guarantees a $K_t$-immersion; and Fox–Pach–Suk (2025) proved the conjecture for graphs on at most $(1.64-o(1))t$ vertices.

since_posted.title: Immersing small complete graphs
since_posted.authors: Matt DeVos, Ken-ichi Kawarabayashi, Bojan Mohar, Haruko Okamura
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for $t \le 7$ via the stronger result that every graph with minimum degree $t-1$ immerses $K_t$.

since_posted.title: Forcing clique immersions through chromatic number
since_posted.authors: Gregory Gauthier, Tien-Nam Le, Paul Wollan
since_posted.claim: Shows that every graph with chromatic number at least $3.54t+4$ immerses $K_t$, a linear sufficient condition weaker than the conjectured threshold of $t$.

since_posted.title: Immersions and Albertson's conjecture
since_posted.authors: Jacob Fox, Janos Pach, Andrew Suk
since_posted.claim: Proves the Abu-Khzam–Langston/Lescure–Meyniel conjecture for graphs on at most $(1.64-o(1))k$ vertices, where $k$ is the chromatic number.

search_queries: chromatic number immersion complete graph conjecture Abu-Khzam Langston
graph immersion conjecture chi(G) Kt complete graph proof 2010 2020 2025
DeVos Kawarabayashi Mohar Okamura immersion coloring graph
immersion conjecture chromatic number Kt solved proved arXiv 2015 2020 2024 2025
notes: The conjecture was independently formulated by Lescure and Meyniel in 1989, predating Abu-Khzam–Langston. No journal publication was located for the Gauthier–Le–Wollan arXiv preprint (1703.08235). The Fox–Pach–Suk (2025) paper targets Albertson's crossing-number conjecture but proves the immersion conjecture as an intermediate result for graphs with at most $(1.64-o(1))k$ vertices; it is an arXiv preprint as of the review date. The conjecture itself remains fully open for all $t$.
